21.correct: Article 213 empowers the Governor of a state to promulgate ordinances when the State Legislature is not in session and immediate action is needed.

wrong.A: Article 200 deals with the Governor's assent to bills.
wrong.D: Article 368 deals with amendment of the Constitution.

21.fact: An ordinance must be laid before the legislature and ceases to operate six weeks after it reassembles.

22.correct: The Gaur dance is typically accompanied by instruments such as the dhol, harmonium and manjira (cymbals), which provide its rhythm.

wrong.B: Sitar and tabla belong to classical music, not the tribal Gaur dance.
wrong.C: Flute and mridangam are classical/Carnatic instruments.
wrong.D: Dholak and shehnai are not the typical Gaur-dance instruments.

22.fact: The Gaur is a vigorous bison-horn dance of the Maria Gonds of the Bastar region.

23.correct: The Natyashastra classifies musical instruments into four categories: tata (stringed), avanaddha (percussion/drums), ghana (solid/cymbals) and sushira (wind).

wrong.A: Three is one short of the four categories.
wrong.C: Five is one more than the classification.

23.fact: The Natyashastra, attributed to Bharata Muni, is the foundational treatise on Indian performing arts.

24.correct: A common bifocal lens has two parts - one concave (for distant vision) and one convex (for near reading) - combined in a single lens.

wrong.A: Two concave lenses would not correct near vision.
wrong.B: Two convex lenses would not correct distant vision for such a defect.
wrong.D: Two cylindrical lenses are used to correct astigmatism, not in standard bifocals.

24.fact: Bifocal lenses were famously invented by Benjamin Franklin.

25.correct: 'The Sari Shop' was written by Rupa Bajwa; it won the Sahitya Akademi Award and other honours.

wrong.A: R.K. Narayan wrote the Malgudi stories.
wrong.B: Anjali Banerjee is a different author.
wrong.D: Shashi Tharoor is known for works like 'The Great Indian Novel'.

25.fact: 'The Sari Shop' (2004) is set in Amritsar and explores class divisions through a shop assistant's eyes.

26.correct: In the Air Quality Index, the 'Moderate' category indicates that air quality may adversely affect the health of sensitive groups (such as people with respiratory issues).

wrong.D: Air being dangerous for everyone corresponds to 'Severe', not Moderate.

26.fact: India's AQI has six categories: Good, Satisfactory, Moderate, Poor, Very Poor and Severe.

27.correct: A geostationary satellite stays fixed above a point on the equator, which requires an orbital period equal to the Earth's rotation - about 24 hours.

wrong.B: 12 hours corresponds to a lower (e.g. GPS-type) orbit.
wrong.C: 6 hours is far too short for geostationary orbit.
wrong.D: 48 hours is twice the Earth's rotation period.

27.fact: Geostationary satellites orbit at about 35,786 km above the equator and are widely used for communication and weather monitoring.

28.correct: Kolkata hosted the final of the Durand Cup 2023 football tournament (at the Vivekananda Yuba Bharati Krirangan, Salt Lake Stadium).

wrong.A: Guwahati hosted some group matches but not the final.
wrong.B: Mumbai did not host the final.
wrong.C: Hyderabad was not the final venue.

28.fact: The Durand Cup is Asia's oldest football tournament, first held in 1888.

29.correct: After the 1991 reforms, the urban service sector expanded rapidly while agriculture stagnated, producing 'growth with inequality' between sectors and regions.

wrong.A: Rural poverty did not fall faster than urban poverty in this pattern.
wrong.B: Agricultural employment did not grow faster than industrial employment.
wrong.C: The informal sector did not decline notably; much employment remained informal.

29.fact: Services now contribute over half of India's GDP, reflecting this services-led growth.

30.correct: Chabahar Port lies near the Strait of Hormuz, giving India easier access to global trade routes and to Afghanistan/Central Asia while bypassing Pakistan.

wrong.A: The Strait of Malacca lies in Southeast Asia, far from Chabahar.
wrong.B: The Strait of Gibraltar is between Europe and Africa.
wrong.D: Bab-el-Mandeb lies at the southern end of the Red Sea.

30.fact: The Strait of Hormuz is one of the world's most important oil-shipping chokepoints.

31.correct: Humans have 46 chromosomes - 23 pairs - of which 22 pairs are autosomes (non-sex chromosomes) and one pair is the sex chromosomes.

wrong.A: 20 is fewer than the number of autosome pairs.
wrong.C: 23 is the total number of pairs, not the number of autosomal pairs.
wrong.D: 21 is one short of the correct number.

31.fact: The 23rd pair (XX or XY) determines biological sex.

32.correct: Post-1991 financial-sector reforms included the entry of private banks, strengthening of capital markets, and deregulation of interest rates - so all of these apply.

wrong.A: Entry of private banks is correct but only one part of the answer.
wrong.B: Strengthening capital markets is correct but not the whole answer.
wrong.C: Deregulation of interest rates is correct but not the complete answer alone.

32.fact: SEBI was given statutory powers in 1992 to regulate and strengthen the capital markets.

33.correct: Sand artist Sudarshan Pattnaik was honoured with the 'Fred Darrington Award for Excellence in Art and Culture' in 2025.

wrong.B: The Dalai Lama is not the recipient of this sand-art award.
wrong.C: Banu Mushtaq is a writer, not the awardee here.
wrong.D: R.K. Sriramkumar is not the recipient.

33.fact: Sudarshan Pattnaik is an internationally renowned sand artist from Odisha.

34.correct: The Godavari is known as the 'Dakshin Ganga' (Ganga of the South) - it is the longest river of peninsular India.

wrong.A: The Krishna is a major southern river but not called Dakshin Ganga.
wrong.C: The Narmada flows westward and is not the Dakshin Ganga.
wrong.D: The Kaveri is the 'Ganga of the South' in Tamil tradition but the standard answer here is the Godavari.

34.fact: The Godavari rises at Trimbak in Maharashtra and flows into the Bay of Bengal.

35.correct: Ahmedabad has historically been called the 'Manchester of India' because of its large cotton-textile industry.

wrong.A: Mumbai-Pune is an industrial belt but not the 'Manchester of India'.
wrong.C: Kanpur is the 'Manchester of North India' / leather city, not the original.
wrong.D: Coimbatore is the 'Manchester of South India'.

35.fact: Ahmedabad's textile mills earned it the nickname after the British textile city of Manchester.

36.correct: The Credit Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme (CLCSS) specifically supports MSMEs for technology upgradation by providing a capital subsidy on the purchase of modern equipment.

wrong.A: PMEGP supports new micro-enterprise creation and self-employment, not technology upgrade subsidies.
wrong.C: 'Make in India' is a broad initiative, not a specific MSME tech-upgrade subsidy.
wrong.D: The Atal Innovation Mission promotes innovation and incubation, not capital subsidies for tech upgrade.

36.fact: CLCSS provides a capital subsidy (commonly 15%) on institutional finance for upgrading plant and machinery.

37.correct: Jainism adopted Prakrit (especially Ardhamagadhi) as its sacred language; Jain scriptures (Agamas) were composed in Prakrit.

wrong.A: Urdu developed much later and is unrelated to ancient Jain texts.
wrong.C: Telugu is a Dravidian language, not the language of Jain scriptures.
wrong.D: Bengali is a later regional language, not ancient Jain Prakrit.

37.fact: Buddhism similarly used Pali, another Prakrit, for its early texts.

38.correct: The three Sangams (assemblies of Tamil poets) of the Sangam Age were held under the patronage of the Pandya kings.

wrong.A: The Gupta kings ruled in the north, unrelated to the Tamil Sangams.
wrong.C: The Mauryan kings did not patronise the Sangams.
wrong.D: The Satavahanas ruled the Deccan, not the Tamil Sangams.

38.fact: Sangam literature is the earliest body of Tamil writing, dating roughly from 300 BCE to 300 CE.

39.correct: Pune became the capital of the Maratha Empire under the Peshwas, who made it the centre of their administration.

wrong.A: Mumbai (Bombay) was a British/Portuguese trading centre, not the Peshwa capital.
wrong.B: Nagpur was the seat of the Bhonsles, not the Peshwa capital.

39.fact: The Peshwas were hereditary prime ministers who became the effective rulers of the Maratha confederacy from Pune.

40.correct: Both the Brahmaputra plains (I) and the Ganga plains (II) are densely populated regions of India, owing to their fertile alluvial soils and abundant water.

wrong.A: 'Only I' is incomplete; the Ganga plains are also densely populated.
wrong.B: 'Only II' is incomplete; the Brahmaputra plains are also densely populated.
wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since both are densely populated.

40.fact: The Indo-Gangetic plain is one of the most densely populated regions in the world.
